reasoning: Query is about staying in Buddhist temple lodging (shukubo). Top hits are unrelated ryokans in Iwate plus Chinese/Traditional translations of Saga prefecture intros. Only one mid-result mentions Nagano Tamashoin shukubo. Cookie-banner descriptions and untranslated CN/TW noise dominate.

improvement_hint: Boost spots whose name/description contains 宿坊 token; demote CN/TW translation duplicates and cookie-policy boilerplate.

reasoning: Query asks for quiet hot-spring villages with yukata strolling. Results are dominated by 温泉神社 (Onsen Shrine) Wikidata entries — accurate but irrelevant ranking. Misses Kinosaki, Ginzan, Kurokawa, Nozawa town-level entries despite their existence in corpus.

reasoning: Query is whale watching in French. Top hit is a whale-shaped fountain monument in Nagoya, then unrelated Kyoto rivers, Monet's Garden Kochi, Saga/Miyazaki tourism intros. No actual whale-watching ports (Ogasawara, Kochi, Wakayama, Okinawa).

improvement_hint: Add whale-watching synonym expansion (ホエールウォッチング, ザトウクジラ) and bias toward coastal municipalities.

reasoning: Heritage call returns Kyoto's '800-Year History of Japanese Tea' story with rich body — directly addresses deep tea-ceremony intent with named places (Uji, Wazuka, Tsuen-chaya, Kosho-ji). Second item (Chinjufu) is irrelevant noise but doesn't dominate.
missing_data: Concrete bookable tea ceremony schools (Urasenke, Omotesenke) for actual experience.

reasoning: Query about mountain villages on infrequent train lines. Returns mostly nav-chrome ('About This Page', cookie banners, generic 'Travel Planning') and one Tsugaru DMO chunk. No specific local lines (Iida Line, Tadami Line, Yonesaka).
missing_data: Iida Line, Tadami Line, Yonesaka Line, JR Hisatsu — specific 1-train-per-2-hour rural lines.

reasoning: Returns full GI-designated foods catalog (507) with descriptions in EN. Useful raw material but ignores 'hasn't gone mainstream' constraint — top entries are mainstream Kobe Beef, Yubari Melon. Agent could filter, but server gave no prominence/obscurity signal.
missing_data: Obscurity / mainstream-popularity signal; lesser-known regional dishes (e.g., narezushi, kabura-zushi).

reasoning: Same as L4-01, JA version. Solid GI catalog but doesn't filter 'メジャーじゃない'. Aomori Cassis, Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef, Yubari Melon top results are very mainstream.
missing_data: Obscurity ranking; non-GI 郷土料理 (郡上味噌、しょっつる、いずし) for true 'unknown' foods.

reasoning: Query is about endangered craftsman skills. Returns Important Intangible CP including Nogaku and folk festivals — these are arts/festivals, not craftsman techniques. Better fit would be 工芸技術 categories.

reasoning: Mt Fuji-themed entries returned correctly (Fuji-san, Sengen Taisha, Fuji-Hakone-Izu, Omuro Sengen) but interleaved with roller coaster and bamboo garden. Hokusai's '36 Views' viewpoints (Oshino, Lake Kawaguchi, Miho-no-Matsubara) not surfaced explicitly.

reasoning: Returns 104 Japan Heritage stories. Lesser-known constraint not honored — comes back in story_id order so famous ones may dominate; first item (educational heritage) is fairly obscure though. Acceptable raw material.

reasoning: Strong hits: Sannai-Maruyama-related Jomon Jiyukan, Komakino Site, Niigata Joumon-no-Sato Asahi, plus Nagano Kuroyou-seki experience museum and Yamanashi Joumon-sanpo. Geographically appropriate (Aomori, Niigata, Nagano, Yamanashi).

reasoning: Tomioka Silk Mill, Kagoshima Shuseikan, Nagoya industrial tourism, Gunma DMO industrial heritage, Kamaishi/Aizu DMO plans — solid coverage of 明治日本の産業革命遺産 themes.

reasoning: Returns 81 Important Intangible Folk CP festivals — all are designated, hence preserved (the opposite of 'dying'). Each entry well-attributed (Kifune, Okumakabuto, Tatemon, Yasurai, Ueno Tenjin). Constraint 'lost or dying' isn't filterable.

reasoning: Excellent: Namie Ukedo elementary school (震災遺構), Kamaishi DMC disaster-learning programs, Rikuzentakata TSUNAMI Memorial Hall, Kesennuma DMO plan. Directly addresses 2011-tsunami-shaped coastal areas. Mixed in is Noto 2024 quake content but explained well.
